name: Cloud Container Escape Detection & Response
description: Detects container escape attempts in Kubernetes or ECS environments. Riggs AI analyzes the alert for privilege escalation indicators such as hostPID, hostNetwork, or privileged containers. Confirmed escapes trigger immediate pod isolation, node investigation to determine blast radius, workload rebuild from clean image, incident ticket creation, and team notification. Unconfirmed alerts receive a severity adjustment and analyst note.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: Kubernetes API
required_integrations.reason: Isolate compromised pods, inspect node status, and rebuild workloads

required_integrations.name: AWS Security Hub
required_integrations.reason: Source of container runtime threat findings and node-level security data

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create incident tickets for confirmed container escape events

required_integrations.name: Slack
required_integrations.reason: Notify the cloud security team of container escape detection and response actions

config.description: Triggers when a container escape attempt or runtime anomaly is detected by Falco, GuardDuty for EKS, or similar container security tooling

config.additional_instructions: Evaluate container escape indicators: hostPID, hostNetwork, privileged flag, nsenter usage, mount of host filesystem
config.description: AI-powered analysis of container runtime alert to determine if a genuine container escape or privilege escalation occurred
Routes based on whether the AI assessment confirmed a container escape event
Immediately isolate the compromised pod by applying a deny-all network policy to prevent lateral movement

config.query: Kubernetes node diagnostics for node={{trigger_1.alert.node_name}}: running processes, mounted volumes, other pods on same node, recent kubectl exec events
config.description: Investigate the underlying host node to determine blast radius — check for other affected pods, suspicious processes, and host-level compromise indicators

parameters.reason: Rebuilding workload after confirmed container escape

config.description: Delete the compromised pod and redeploy the workload from a clean, verified container image
Create an incident ticket with full details of the container escape and all response actions taken
Send Slack notification to the cloud security team with escape details and remediation status

parameters.note: Reviewed by Riggs AI (priv_escalation template). Container escape not confirmed — likely benign runtime anomaly. Confidence: {{analyze_1.confidence}}. Assessment: {{analyze_1.summary}}

config.description: Document that AI analysis did not confirm a container escape and attach assessment details
Container escape detection and response complete
